questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A city police department wants to search the apartment of a person suspected of stealing electronics. Before the search, officers present evidence to a judge. The judge decides whether there is probable cause and, if so, issues a warrant that describes the place to be searched and the items being sought.
Which constitutional protection is most directly illustrated by this process?

choices.A: Protection against unreasonable searches and seizures
choices.B: Protection against excessive bail
choices.C: Right to vote in federal elections
choices.D: Right to assemble peacefully

questions.correctAnswerText: Protection against unreasonable searches and seizures
questions.explanation: The warrant process helps protect people from unreasonable government searches.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
Under the Articles of Confederation, the national government had difficulty raising revenue and enforcing laws. When economic unrest broke out in Massachusetts in the 1780s, some leaders argued that the country needed a stronger national government. Others worried that increasing national power could threaten liberty.
Which issue does this source best show?

choices.A: The debate over whether slavery should expand westward
choices.B: The weakness of the first national government
choices.C: The success of direct democracy in every state
choices.D: The end of disputes between farmers and merchants

questions.correctAnswerText: The weakness of the first national government
questions.explanation: The source describes difficulties under the Articles of Confederation that led some leaders to seek a stronger national government.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A local market tracked the price and quantity sold of fresh strawberries.
Price per basket: $5, $4, $3
Baskets sold per day: 120, 170, 230
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Lower prices were associated with more baskets sold.
choices.B: Higher prices were associated with more baskets sold.
choices.C: The number of baskets sold stayed the same at every price.
choices.D: Price had no relationship to sales in the table.

questions.correctAnswerText: Lower prices were associated with more baskets sold.
questions.explanation: As the price fell from $5 to $3, the number of baskets sold rose from 120 to 230.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A family leaves a rural area after several years of crop failures and moves to a city where relatives say factories are hiring. The family expects steadier wages and better access to schools.
Which statement best identifies the push and pull factors in this example?

choices.A: Crop failures are a pull factor, and factory jobs are a push factor.
choices.B: Crop failures are a push factor, and factory jobs are a pull factor.
choices.C: Schools are a push factor, and crop failures are a pull factor.
choices.D: Relatives are the only factor affecting migration.

questions.correctAnswerText: Crop failures are a push factor, and factory jobs are a pull factor.
questions.explanation: Crop failures push the family away, while jobs pull the family toward the city.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
The Constitution gives Congress the power to impeach and remove certain federal officials. It gives the president the power to appoint federal judges with Senate approval.
Which TWO statements best explain how these powers reflect constitutional design?

choices.A: They create ways for one branch to limit another branch.
choices.B: They allow every state to ignore federal courts.
choices.C: They prevent federal officials from being completely unchecked.
choices.D: They eliminate the need for elections.
choices.E: They give the president sole control over Congress.

questions.correctAnswerText: They create ways for one branch to limit another branch. | They prevent federal officials from being completely unchecked.
questions.explanation: Impeachment, removal, appointments, and Senate approval are checks that limit concentrated power.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Paired sources:
Source A:
A writer supports the proposed Constitution, arguing that a larger republic can better control factions because many different interests will compete. According to this view, no single group will easily dominate the whole country.
Source B:
A critic worries that a distant national government may not understand local needs. The critic argues that strong protections are needed so individual rights and state authority are not swallowed by national power.
What is the main disagreement between the two sources?

choices.A: Whether government should collect any taxes at all
choices.B: Whether a stronger national government would protect or threaten liberty
choices.C: Whether the colonies should remain part of Britain
choices.D: Whether courts should be abolished

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether a stronger national government would protect or threaten liberty
questions.explanation: Source A favors a larger republic, while Source B worries about national power threatening rights and state authority.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
In 1803, the United States purchased a large territory west of the Mississippi River. The purchase doubled the nation’s land area and opened new opportunities for settlement and trade. Some political leaders supported the purchase, while others questioned whether the Constitution clearly gave the president power to acquire new territory.
Which issue is most clearly reflected in this source?

choices.A: Debate over the constitutional power of the federal government
choices.B: Debate over whether railroads should be regulated
choices.C: Conflict over joining the United Nations
choices.D: Conflict over ending the income tax

questions.correctAnswerText: Debate over the constitutional power of the federal government
questions.explanation: The source describes disagreement over whether the Constitution allowed the president to acquire territory.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: An amendment to the U.S. Constitution is usually proposed by [D1] and must be ratified by [D2].

D1: two-thirds of both houses of Congress

questions.correct: D1=two-thirds of both houses of Congress; D2=three-fourths of the states
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=two-thirds of both houses of Congress; D2=three-fourths of the states
questions.explanation: Most amendments are proposed by two-thirds of both houses of Congress and ratified by three-fourths of the states.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A country has 12,000,000 people and 300,000 square miles of land. What is the population density in people per square mile?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: 12,000,000 divided by 300,000 equals 40 people per square mile.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A government places a tariff on imported steel. Domestic steel companies support the tariff because it may make foreign steel more expensive. Some manufacturers oppose it because they use steel to make cars and appliances.
Which conclusion is best supported by this scenario?

choices.A: Trade policies can benefit some groups while raising costs for others.
choices.B: Tariffs always lower production costs for every business.
choices.C: Imported goods are never affected by government policy.
choices.D: Manufacturers do not depend on raw materials.

questions.correctAnswerText: Trade policies can benefit some groups while raising costs for others.
questions.explanation: The tariff may help domestic steel companies but raise costs for manufacturers that buy steel.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A state university admits students to a public program. A rejected applicant claims that the admissions rule treats applicants differently without a valid public reason. The case reaches a federal court, where judges consider whether the rule violates the Equal Protection Clause.
Which constitutional principle is the court most likely examining?

choices.A: Fair treatment under the law
choices.B: The power to declare war
choices.C: The right of states to coin money
choices.D: The president’s power to appoint ambassadors

questions.correctAnswerText: Fair treatment under the law
questions.explanation: Equal protection concerns whether the government treats people fairly under the law.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A historian studies migration from the rural South to Northern and Western cities.
Period: 1910–1920, 1920–1930, 1940–1950
Estimated migrants: 500,000, 800,000, 1,500,000
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Migration decreased in every period listed.
choices.B: Migration was highest during 1910–1920.
choices.C: Migration increased across the listed periods.
choices.D: No migration occurred after 1940.

questions.correctAnswerText: Migration increased across the listed periods.
questions.explanation: The estimated number of migrants increased from 500,000 to 800,000 to 1,500,000.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
After a hurricane, local officials organize shelters, the state government sends emergency crews, and the federal government provides disaster aid and coordination. Each level of government has a role in the response.
Which constitutional principle is illustrated by this example?

choices.A: Federalism
choices.B: Judicial review
choices.C: Executive privilege
choices.D: Direct taxation

questions.correctAnswerText: Federalism
questions.explanation: Federalism divides responsibilities among local, state, and national governments.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A port city grows quickly because it has a deep harbor, nearby rail lines, and access to international shipping routes. Over time, warehouses, factories, and immigrant neighborhoods develop near the waterfront.
Which conclusion is best supported by the scenario?

choices.A: Physical location and transportation links can shape urban growth.
choices.B: Cities grow only when farming becomes impossible.
choices.C: International trade prevents population growth.
choices.D: Waterfront areas cannot support industry.

questions.correctAnswerText: Physical location and transportation links can shape urban growth.
questions.explanation: The city grows because of its harbor, rail lines, and shipping access.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A jury listens to evidence in a criminal trial. Jurors must decide whether the government has proven the charge beyond a reasonable doubt. The judge explains that the accused person is presumed innocent unless the government meets this burden.
Which civic principle is most closely related to this procedure?

choices.A: Protection of the rights of the accused
choices.B: Popular election of judges
choices.C: Expansion of executive power
choices.D: State control of foreign policy

questions.correctAnswerText: Protection of the rights of the accused
questions.explanation: Presumption of innocence and proof beyond a reasonable doubt protect accused persons in criminal trials.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
In 1848, reformers at a women’s rights convention argued that women should have greater legal rights, educational opportunities, and political voice. Their declaration used language similar to founding-era documents to claim that rights should apply more equally.
Which TWO conclusions are best supported by this description?

choices.A: Reformers connected their demands to American ideals of equality.
choices.B: Reformers argued that social and legal institutions could be changed.
choices.C: Reformers demanded an immediate end to all political parties.
choices.D: Reformers opposed all forms of public education.
choices.E: Reformers believed voting rights should be limited to property owners.

questions.correctAnswerText: Reformers connected their demands to American ideals of equality. | Reformers argued that social and legal institutions could be changed.
questions.explanation: The description shows reformers using founding ideals to argue for changes in rights and institutions.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A worker’s monthly rent changed over three years.
Year: 2021, 2022, 2023
Monthly rent: $900, $990, $1,080
From 2021 to 2023, by how many dollars did the monthly rent increase?
questions.explanation: $1,080 minus $900 equals a $180 increase.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A state legislature passes a law regulating restaurant health inspections. A city then passes a more detailed ordinance requiring restaurants to post inspection grades at the entrance. The city says the ordinance addresses local public health concerns and does not conflict with state law.
Which idea best explains this situation?

choices.A: Local governments may exercise authority granted by state law.
choices.B: Cities can declare war when public safety is involved.
choices.C: Federal courts write all local ordinances.
choices.D: State governments have no role in public health.

questions.correctAnswerText: Local governments may exercise authority granted by state law.
questions.explanation: Cities often pass ordinances under authority provided by state law, as long as they do not conflict with higher law.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A region reports the following freshwater use.
Sector: Agriculture, Industry, Households
Water use: 62%, 24%, 14%
Which sector uses the smallest share of freshwater?

choices.A: Agriculture
choices.B: Industry
choices.C: Households
choices.D: Agriculture and Industry equally

questions.correctAnswerText: Households
questions.explanation: Households use 14%, which is the smallest share listed.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt: During the Cold War, the United States often tried to limit the spread of communism. This policy was known as [D1], and it shaped U.S. actions in places such as [D2].

D2: the Erie Canal and Cumberland Road

questions.correctAnswerText: D1=containment; D2=Korea and Vietnam
questions.explanation: Containment was the policy of limiting the spread of communism and shaped U.S. actions in Korea and Vietnam.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A town election had the following results.
Candidate: Rivera, Chen, Patel, Morgan
Votes: 3,450, 2,980, 1,720, 850
How many total votes were cast for the four candidates?

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
A state requires citizens to register before voting. The registration form asks for name, address, age, and citizenship status. Election officials use the information to confirm that each person is eligible and assigned to the correct voting location.
What is the main purpose of this registration system?

choices.A: To help election officials determine voter eligibility and polling location
choices.B: To allow political parties to control every ballot
choices.C: To require voters to explain their opinions before voting
choices.D: To replace secret ballots with public voting

questions.correctAnswerText: To help election officials determine voter eligibility and polling location
questions.explanation: The form confirms voter eligibility and assigns voters to the proper location.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A household has $200 left after paying required bills. The family can use the money for an emergency savings account, new furniture, or entertainment. They choose to save the money because the car may need repairs soon.
Which economic concept is most clearly shown?

choices.A: Opportunity cost
choices.B: Judicial review
choices.C: Naturalization
choices.D: Population density

questions.correctAnswerText: Opportunity cost
questions.explanation: Choosing savings means giving up other possible uses of the $200.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
Source:
During the 1950s and 1960s, civil rights activists used lawsuits, boycotts, marches, and voter registration campaigns to challenge segregation and discrimination. Many activists argued that constitutional promises of equal protection had not been fully honored.
Which statement best explains the activists’ main argument?

choices.A: Existing democratic principles should be applied more fully and fairly.
choices.B: The Constitution had no connection to civil rights claims.
choices.C: Segregation had already ended everywhere before activism began.
choices.D: Courts and public protest were never used by civil rights activists.

questions.correctAnswerText: Existing democratic principles should be applied more fully and fairly.
questions.explanation: The activists appealed to equal protection and democratic rights to challenge discrimination.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.
questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Judicial branch; T2=Executive branch; T3=Legislative branch
questions.explanation: The judicial branch interprets laws, the executive branch enforces laws, and the legislative branch makes laws.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A citizen group collects signatures asking the city to add a public transportation question to the next local ballot. The group argues that voters should be able to decide whether to approve funding for the project.
Which form of civic participation is described?

choices.A: Petitioning for a ballot measure
choices.B: Serving on a federal court
choices.C: Issuing an executive order
choices.D: Conducting a criminal trial

questions.correctAnswerText: Petitioning for a ballot measure
questions.explanation: Collecting signatures to place an issue on the ballot is a form of petitioning and civic participation.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A government plans a new highway through a mountain region. Supporters say it will improve trade and access to hospitals. Critics warn that it may damage forests and disrupt communities that depend on local tourism.
Which statement best summarizes the geographic issue?

choices.A: Infrastructure decisions can create both economic benefits and environmental or social costs.
choices.B: Mountain regions never need transportation systems.
choices.C: Tourism cannot be affected by land-use decisions.
choices.D: Highways eliminate all environmental concerns.

questions.correctAnswerText: Infrastructure decisions can create both economic benefits and environmental or social costs.
questions.explanation: The highway may improve access and trade but also harm forests and local tourism communities.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A senator refuses a gift from a company that is affected by a bill the senator will vote on. The senator explains that public officials should avoid conflicts between private benefit and public duty.
Which civic principle is best reflected in the senator’s action?

choices.A: Ethical conduct in public service
choices.B: Trial by jury
choices.C: Popular referendum
choices.D: Military command authority

questions.correctAnswerText: Ethical conduct in public service
questions.explanation: Avoiding a conflict of interest reflects ethical behavior by a public official.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
During the Great Depression, unemployment rose sharply, banks failed, and many families lost savings and homes. The crisis led many Americans to reconsider the role of the federal government in economic life.
Which change was most closely connected to this period?

choices.A: Expansion of federal programs designed to provide relief and recovery
choices.B: Immediate end of all federal economic policy
choices.C: Complete elimination of state governments
choices.D: Replacement of elections with hereditary rule

questions.correctAnswerText: Expansion of federal programs designed to provide relief and recovery
questions.explanation: The Great Depression contributed to expanded federal relief and recovery programs.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A city spent $48 million on road repairs last year and $60 million this year. What was the percent increase in spending?
Type your answer as a whole number percent without the percent sign.
questions.explanation: Spending increased by $12 million; $12 million divided by $48 million equals 25%.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A resident attends a town hall meeting and says, “I disagree with the mayor’s proposal, but I respect that the decision should be made through open debate and a lawful vote.”
Which democratic value is best shown by this statement?

choices.A: Respect for peaceful disagreement and lawful decision-making
choices.B: Rejection of all public discussion
choices.C: Support for rule by a single official
choices.D: Opposition to voting in local government

questions.correctAnswerText: Respect for peaceful disagreement and lawful decision-making
questions.explanation: The statement supports debate, disagreement, and decisions made through lawful procedures.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A county survey asked residents which public service should receive increased funding.
Service: Road repair, Public parks, Fire services, Libraries
Responses: 420, 260, 390, 130
Which TWO statements are supported by the data?

choices.A: Road repair received the most responses.
choices.B: Libraries received more responses than fire services.
choices.C: Fire services received 390 responses.
choices.D: Public parks received fewer responses than libraries.
choices.E: Road repair and fire services together received 810 responses.

questions.correctAnswerText: Road repair received the most responses. | Fire services received 390 responses.
questions.explanation: Road repair has the highest number at 420, and fire services has 390 responses.

questions.skill: Reading for Meaning in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
Source:
The Constitution requires that the president be a natural-born citizen, at least 35 years old, and a resident of the United States for at least 14 years. These requirements apply before a person can be elected president.
What is the main purpose of this constitutional provision?

choices.A: To set eligibility rules for holding the presidency
choices.B: To decide how federal judges are confirmed
choices.C: To describe how states ratify amendments
choices.D: To give Congress power to borrow money

questions.correctAnswerText: To set eligibility rules for holding the presidency
questions.explanation: The listed age, citizenship, and residency requirements define who may serve as president.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphs in Social Studies
questions.prompt:
A table shows changes in U.S. manufacturing employment in a city.
Year: 1970, 1980, 1990
Manufacturing jobs: 82,000, 61,000, 44,000
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Manufacturing jobs increased throughout the period.
choices.B: Manufacturing jobs decreased from 1970 to 1990.
choices.C: Manufacturing jobs were highest in 1990.
choices.D: Manufacturing jobs stayed exactly the same.

questions.correctAnswerText: Manufacturing jobs decreased from 1970 to 1990.
questions.explanation: Manufacturing jobs fell from 82,000 in 1970 to 44,000 in 1990.

questions.skill: Analyzing Historical Events and Arguments
questions.prompt:
A court rules that a public school may not punish students simply for expressing an unpopular opinion in a peaceful way, as long as the expression does not seriously disrupt the school environment.
Which constitutional idea is most closely connected to the ruling?

choices.A: Free expression is protected, though it may have reasonable limits.
choices.B: Students have no constitutional rights in public schools.
choices.C: Only adults may express political opinions.
choices.D: Schools may punish any opinion that most people dislike.

questions.correctAnswerText: Free expression is protected, though it may have reasonable limits.
questions.explanation: The ruling protects peaceful expression but recognizes limits when serious disruption occurs.
